verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 is a type I integral membrane protein with canonical trans-Golgi network localization, but documented literature shows it actively cycles between the TGN and the cell surface via endosomes. Although the TGN is the dominant steady-state compartment, the protein regularly transits the plasma membrane as part of its normal trafficking cycle. It reaches the outer face via its own single-pass TM domain, not through transient recruitment, qualifying for surface accessibility under dual-localization kinetics.
